children.text: What is the relationship that exists between winds at 2 000 feet above the surface and the surface winds?

answers: The winds at 2 000 feet and at the surface flow in the same direction but the surface winds are weaker due to friction.
The winds at 2 000 feet are not effected by Coriolis force.
The surface winds tend to veer as compared to the winds at 2 000 feet.
The winds at 2 000 feet tend to parallel the isobars while the surface winds cross the isobars at an angle towards the lower pressure and are weaker.
correctAnswer: The winds at 2 000 feet tend to parallel the isobars while the surface winds cross the isobars at an angle towards the lower pressure and are weaker.

children.text: Surface winds flow across the isobars at an angle rather than parallel to the isobars due to

answers: the greater density of the air near the surface.

covers an extensive area and has fairly uniform properties of temperature and moisture.
correctAnswer: covers an extensive area and has fairly uniform properties of temperature and moisture.
